name: Z-Score Calculator
description: Industrial-grade Z-score calculator for process capability analysis, outlier detection, and statistical process control (SPC) based on ISO 3534-2 and Six Sigma standards.

inputs.label: Data Points (comma-separated)
inputs.businessContext: Raw sample measurements from the process. At least 2 values required for calculation.

inputs.label: Population Mean (μ)
inputs.businessContext: Target or historical mean of the process. If unknown, leave blank to use sample mean.

inputs.label: Population Standard Deviation (σ)
inputs.businessContext: Known process standard deviation. If unknown, leave blank to use sample standard deviation.

inputs.label: Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Desired confidence level for interval estimation. Common values: 90%, 95%, 99%.

inputs.label: Tail Type
inputs.businessContext: Specifies whether the test is one-tailed (upper/lower) or two-tailed.

inputs.label: Use Sample Standard Deviation (if σ unknown)
inputs.businessContext: When true, the calculator will estimate σ from the sample data using Bessel's correction.

rules.message: At least two data points are required to compute statistics.
Population standard deviation must be greater than zero.

rules.condition: (population_mean != null && population_stddev == null) || (population_mean == null && population_stddev != null)
rules.message: Both population mean and standard deviation must be provided together, or both left blank.

thresholds.message: |Z-score| > 3 indicates a potential outlier or out-of-control process (Six Sigma rule).
Process may be operating outside acceptable capability limits (Cp < 1.0).
Confidence interval is wide relative to the mean; consider increasing sample size.

formulas.name: Sample Mean (x̄)
formulas.description: Arithmetic mean of the input data points.

formulas.name: Sample Standard Deviation (s)
formulas.description: Unbiased estimate of population standard deviation using Bessel's correction.

formulas.name: Effective Standard Deviation
formulas.description: Standard deviation used in Z-score calculation: population σ if provided, else sample s.

formulas.name: Effective Mean
formulas.description: Mean used in Z-score calculation: population μ if provided, else sample mean.

formulas.name: Z-Score (Primary Result)
formulas.description: Standardized score indicating how many standard deviations a data point (or sample mean) is from the population mean.

formulas.name: P-Value
formulas.description: Probability of observing a Z-score as extreme as the calculated value under the null hypothesis.
formulas.formula: p = 2 * (1 - Φ(|Z|)) for two-tailed; p = 1 - Φ(Z) for upper-tailed; p = Φ(Z) for lower-tailed

formulas.name: Confidence Interval for the Mean
formulas.description: Range within which the true population mean is expected to lie with the specified confidence level.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Small Sample Size
hiddenLossDrivers.description: n < 30 reduces reliability of Z-test; consider using t-distribution.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Variability
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Coefficient of variation > 0.5 indicates excessive process spread.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Non-Normal Data
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Z-test assumes normality; skewness or kurtosis may bias results.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Outlier Presence
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Data points with |Z| > 3 may distort mean and standard deviation.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Sample Size
suggestedActions.description: Collect more data to improve estimate precision and reduce confidence interval width.

suggestedActions.label: Check Process Stability
suggestedActions.description: Use control charts (e.g., X-bar-R) to verify process is in statistical control.

suggestedActions.label: Transform Data
suggestedActions.description: If data is non-normal, apply Box-Cox or Johnson transformation before Z-test.

suggestedActions.label: Use t-Test Instead
suggestedActions.description: For small samples (n < 30) with unknown σ, switch to one-sample t-test.

suggestedActions.label: Investigate Outliers
suggestedActions.description: Review data points with |Z| > 3 for assignable causes and consider removal if justified.
